change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Pete Hest North American Indian collection
did.abstract: Promotional and educational published material, pamphlets, brochures, photographs, posters, scrapbooks and other material collected by Pete Hest, relating to the history and culture of Native Americans in the United States and Canada.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Promotional and educational published material, pamphlets, brochures, photographs, posters, scrapbooks and other material collected by Pete Hest, relating to the history and culture of Native Americans in the United States and Canada.

odd.head: NOTES
odd.p: Forms part of the Native American Collection. (NAC)

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Pete Hest North American Indian collection, #6507. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Hopi Snake Dancer circa 1900, copy print
Onodaga - Chief F. Henhawk, Edson-Thomas, Chief George Thomas, Sky-Yah-Deh, Edson Thomas
Ha-Do-Ee - Chief Floyd Henhawk, Chief J. Cornplanter, Cayuga Village, Onondaga, NYS Fair, Craft shop
Chief HighEagle - Age 98- "Ogala" Sioux taken 1953, Concho, Oklahoma
Chief Koon-Kah-Za-Chy addressing the council in sign language
Misc. prints, NYS Fair, Pete Hest, unidentified ceremonies and events
Misc. prints of artifacts from the "Indianer Museum Radebeul", Germany, and other artifacts
Group and individual portraits of Native Americans
Dr. Erl Bates giving address at the 150th Anniversary of the Battle of Chestnut Woods, Grahamnsville, NY 1925
Six Nation Meeting, Onondaga Reserve, 1953, N.Y.S. Fair 1952, Planting Ceremony 1953, other misc. prints
N.Y.S. Fair, Totem Village, Cooperstown, NY
Misc. portraits - Pete Hest, Native Americans

Philip Green, Albert Cusick, Melinda Johnson, 1890
Taos, NM, John Kilhan and unidentified Native Americans
Thunder Bird's troupe of Wild Indians
Chief Thunder Bird amd his Wild Indians, Long Beach CA
Tim McCoy captured by the Indians in Metro-Goldwyn Mayers "Sioux Blood".
Mrs. Margaret Thomas, Irving Powless Sr., Ross Webster, George Thomas Jr. Pete Hest
Chief Howard Sky, Grand River, Canada, 1952
Ernest Pierce, Davis Green, George Thomas, Andrew Pierce, Jordan, NY 1934
E.H. Allen, Wm. Eagle Shirt, T.H. Inch, W.G. Brooks
Frank Bald Eagle, Manderson, So. Dak.
Scrapbooks and oversized prints, posters and advertising
